page: Microsoft Azure F4s is an instance in the FS family, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 16 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. No GPU resources are included.

Benchmark data indicates that the F4s sits primarily in the lower performance tiers for raw compute. Its stress-ng single-core performance of 980.09 is in the bottom 10%, while its multi-core score of 4101.05 falls in the bottom 25%. Similarly, Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores are in the bottom 25%. However, the instance shows average performance in specific workloads. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks reaches 359.78 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations perform at an average tier with 5.99 million operations per second. Passmark database operations and LLM inference for a small 135M model also register in the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F4s offers a dedicated core configuration with a lower memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it an entry-level option for compute-constrained tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. Tradeoffs include weak large-block memory bandwidth and lower single-threaded compression speeds. This instance is suitable for light database operations, basic web serving, Redis caching, and low-intensity background processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure F4s is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance from the FS family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor clocked at 2.3 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM and 16 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. Benchmark results place its raw compute performance in the lower tiers, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core scores in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. However, it maintains average performance in memory bandwidth for small cached blocks and Redis SET operations. This instance presents an entry-level, dedicated-core option for workloads with low memory requirements, making it suitable for light database operations, basic web serving, and caching.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F4s is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 16 GB of local storage, suitable for light database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F4s is a 4-vCPU, x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8.0 GB RAM and 16 GB local storage, running on Microsoft Hyper-V.
tagline: An entry-level, dedicated-core compute instance with balanced cache performance for light database operations and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and cores
8.0 GB of system memory and 16 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage
Average performance in small-block cached memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations
Lower-tier raw compute and large-block memory bandwidth performance
Appropriate for light database operations, web serving, and caching
